Silver Charm of Tranquility  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: NECK WRIST
AC: 35
STR: +10 STA: +15 HP: +100 MANA: +100
SV FIRE: +8 SV DISEASE: +8 SV COLD: +8 SV MAGIC: +8 SV POISON: +8
WT: 0.5 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Silver Charm of Tranquility
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: NECK WRIST
AC: 35
STR: +10 STA: +15 HP: +100 MANA: +100
SV FIRE: +8 SV DISEASE: +8 SV COLD: +8 SV MAGIC: +8 SV POISON: +8
WT: 0.5 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Monk
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 10:03:33
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

*This item is the result of a raid event.
Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Test of the Ruby Tear

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Temple of Veeshan


Uploaded November 5th, 2022 by halfridge
Updated November 6th, 2022

Post Comment
best for anyone who does the quest
QuoteReply
# May 06 2003 at 10:41 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781123020
44 posts
This item doesnt drop. It is a major time sink quest. there are 2 realistic ways one completes this quest. Either they are in a guild that is farming HoT which means they need to collect the symbols + tear as per how that guild does the loot to include random cause I seen many guilds in HoT using 1-1k rolls there. Or they get one very strong crew together and they get the parts themselves leaving only Doz as a problem to get the tear(s). All theese quests are all/all and whom ever decides to spend the time to do them deserves the item. Now if you are in a guild that allowed you to loot symbols but when it comes time for the turn in and one has an item to MQ if that guild says this item goes to so and so you are not in a good guild at all. This neck item will blow away all other neck items until you get into VT or better for melee. Casters may have another perspective since they can get nice stat neck pieces with FT/Mana pres or focus effects on them. How ever if you are in a guild that is going to be heading to VT in the futre I would just hold out for Talisman of Van Kerra.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 18 2004 at 5:09 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) well, not to bad for infoz i'd say but a few things i don't agree with. For one its Talisman of Vah Kerrath
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 07 2003 at 12:03 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is no doubt best for a war or a monk. 15 STR/stam and 100 life is good for war, but the resists and strength greatly favor monks
RE: best for monk or war

# May 03 2003 at 10:54 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247140349
112 posts
The usual best for, manure...
and as usual is best for EVERY class that doesn thave better since EVERY class can wear it.

I know my 65 Beast would kill have a 35AC neckpiece, let alone add in 15Sta/100HP/100Mana
thats actually 160HPs....
Tell me again just how this would be "better" for a MONK, than say a Beast/SK/Pally/Ranger?
ALL of which could use 35AC/15Sta/160HPs every bit as much AND make use of the 100Mana....

In reality if your trying to put this on whom would this be truely BEST for, a Monk would be somewhere on down the list no higher than 6th since they cant use the 100mana, and ALL the other plate/chain classes and Beast can use the other stats every bit as much.

# Jul 15 2003 at 3:04 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788855623
274 posts
Don't forget clers. Especially tanking clers, or, even moreso, High Elven clers. My cleric suffers from such low str that he struggles to carry his own weight and armor, let alone any loot. Just today I gated to Kelethin to bank, and I was weighed down so much it took me what seemed like at least 5 minutes to get there and put THREE THINGS in there. THREE THINGS encumbered me. Now I have enough for ONE of them.

I'd love this little thing. And, of course, I could always use more mana, and the HP would be awesome for when I'm aggro'd on. Pally likely first, Cleric second :P Ranger down low, cuz they need more dex for da bows.
▲ Page top
#Thirdday, Posted: Mar 25 2004 at 6:48 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) [Green][i][b]I'm sorry....I'm not into flaming....but if you are into this item just for strength, then you are beyond hope (as far as strength itself is concerned). I have three words for you....Weight reduction bags. Either save your money and go buy a "bag of the tinker" or three, work on a tradeskill, get your trophy, and have unlimitted no rents (plus one permanent one with a SUPER SIMPLE turn in upstairs in the bazaar npc vendor building), do quest for rallic pack and either buy or farm the evil eye bag and/or buddy up with a halfling who makes the other 100% weight reduction bag (who's name escapes me at the moment, because I literally just woke up).

You post this saying pure Melee should be the last to do this quest, but you have yet to see the item. It has had 100 Endurance added to it. Now all these flames about a Warrior getting an Item with Mana are Mute cause most of said Items now have an equal amount of Endurance to them. And putting Endurance on a Caster is a waste of Endurance just as Mana is wasted on pure Melee. And also note, that this Item is Neck/Wrist wearable. This is far better than my Ornate Bracer and will be on my wrist tomarrow when I do my turn ins.

▲ Page top
once and for all!
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2001 at 3:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248837442
95 posts
mainhand swings every time the delay recycles.
for wurmy that would be every 4 seconds. PERIOD.

Dual wield is checked every time the pffhand delay recycles, for lammy thats every 1.9 seconds. PERIOD.

so in 4 seconds you will swingonce with the wurm and twice with the lammy (if you dont fail on dual wield check). PERIOD.

offhand IS NOT LINKED to mainhand. it WILL swing, even if your mainhand misses or isnt recycled yet. PERIOD.

try it out: take a fire beetle eye in mainhand, and a weapon in offhand. go fight something green.
your weapon WILL SWING, even though your mainhand will NOT cause it holds a fire beetle eye.

THATS how it works. P E R I O D.

Edited, Sun Dec 30 03:25:18 2001
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 30 2001 at 1:00 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I don´t get why ppl post ******** like that, do they get a kick out of it?
RE: delay
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2001 at 6:33 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766848380
26 posts
Sorry, little brother, you're completely wrong. At first your offhand IS hooked to your primary, but once you get higher skill in dual wield, the hands become unhooked. The general rule of thumb is delay over anything in the main, and ratio in the off. You see, you need to play the percentages. Every hit you make with your primary hand ONLY gets a damage bonus. This means that a 1/5 weapon in the primary would smack the crap out of any other weapon in existence. In the offhand however, a damage bonus is not added, so you want the best ratio you can get there. This is one of the reasons Wurmslayer isn't quite as godlike as it seems. It may be 25/40, yes, which is an excellent ratio, but it's primary only. This means that although you hit hard, you will actually do more damage overall with a Lamentation which is 9/19, or a Jade Mace which is 9/18 in your primary hand. Just clearing up any confusion and helping the generally uninformed. Laters.
▲ Page top
RE: delay
QuoteReply
# May 19 2002 at 3:21 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591761991555
Scholar
300 posts
First off, the whole linked idea seems deeply flawed. If I understand correctly, if the hands are linked, your offhand can only swing when the primary cycles. However, if you were to put, say, a Jade Mace offand (9/18), with an Icetooth Claw primary (11/29), you'd notice the mace swing independently of the claw. If they were linked, you would never see the mace swing without the claw also swinging.

Secondly, the term damage bonus is thrown around a lot by people using it in two different ways. The damage bonus shown on a weapon (post-50) is only applied to the primary hand. At level 51, all 1H weapons have a damage bonus of 9, so you'll never hit for less than 10 with a weapon in the primary hand (a hit is for at least 1 damage, 1+9=10). However, you can hit for 1 with an offhand weapon, even though the damage bonus is also listed on that weapon. The other (incorrect) way people use the term damage bonus is in the calculation for damage. For example, at level 30 an 11 damage weapon may max at 39 (give or take a couple points for STR). However, at level 49 that weapon may max at 49. Don't flame me, that was just an example for illustration purposes. No damage bonus is applied, per se. Your maximum damage is simply raised a bit as you gain levels.
▲ Page top

# Aug 16 2001 at 9:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592790120983
Scholar
65 posts
Offhand and primary are not linked. Dual weild checks are made by a timer based on your 2nd weapons delay.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Sep 23 2001 at 11:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) actually there is no such thing as offhand delay...the roll to see if your duel weild and your duel attack are rolled each time your prime hand swings...a wurm in prime and lammy in offhand is a great example of this as you will only see the dagger peirce once the wurm has slashed....just try it if you dont belive me....get some high damage/worthless delay weapon in you offhand and a low delay weapon in your prime...you will see you do much more damage and are much faster

#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 20 2001 at 7:37 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hey there Gom... but if I am not mistaken, that piece says ALL / ALL. That means a wizard can put it to JUST as good of use as a warrior. You know, if you think by class, then you are blind to the fact that AC is one of the most important stats in the game, if not, THE most important stat. Even for a strict caster. When you say that a warrior can put it to use, take a good look at exactly what classes Verant says can use it, and when I speak my mind abd when I roll on the item... keep your keyboard in check... you'll end up on my ignore list real fast that way, especially with an arguement as lame as 'warriors need!' QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 12:49 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591757464810
156 posts
You must have vision beyond anyone else then because Warriors don't get Dual Weild until level 13 and the only class that gets it at or before level 6 is a monk.

You did NOT see a level 6 with a Wurmslayer in primary and a Lamentation in secondary. That is unless warriors get Dual Weild at level 6 now or Monks can use 1HS weapons formerly unavailable to them.

If you're going to tell a story to make a point, at least get your facts straight first. Don't exagerate so much. Crafted/Cobalt with a Wurmslawyer OR Lamentation at level 6 would have been plenty.

Either that or go back and edit your post to read:

"Maybe it's just me, but heavy twinking is unbalancing enough with level 13's running around in Crafted/Cobalt w/ a Wurmy for Pri and a Lammy in Sec. Yes, I have seen this."

Actually this particular item is quested by gathering items from mobs in the East wing (Halls of Testing). You don't need to go to North wing at all, and for someone on Dragon Faction (to be able to do this quest) you really don't want to be killing in North wing and taking -CoV faction hits. For this item I might even consider becoming a dragon hugger for a few weeks ... nah.
